task: 최신 reference refresh 후보 중 뼈대 프로젝트에 흡수할 최우선 기능 2개 선정: 기준은 구버전 뼈대 이식/업그레이드 능력, 운영 추적성, 검증 가능성, 최소 침습성

content: Initial ranking: top candidates are component-level install/diff manifest and reference refresh apply mode. Reason: old skeleton upgrade succeeds only when we can compare installed component surfaces and refresh the source registry reproducibly. Skills bloat, source lifecycle, and generated artifact gates matter, but they depend on knowing what component set and reference baseline are being upgraded.

Researcher view: component-level install/diff manifest should be rank 1 because it directly answers what will change when a newer skeleton is overlaid onto an older project. It can point to files, hashes, maturity tiers, generated outputs, and validation expectations. Rank 2 is generated artifact policy gate, because generated surfaces like .codex/.claude/.mcp.json are a recurring transplant risk if stale or edited directly.

Verifier view: the most testable pair is component-level install/diff manifest plus generated artifact policy gate. Both can be validated locally with deterministic file inventories, hashes, ownership classes, and schema checks. Reference refresh apply mode depends on network/upstream state and is less stable as a core upgrade primitive.

Critic challenge to Codex initial ranking: reference refresh apply mode is useful for maintaining this repo, but it is not the strongest transplant primitive. It tells us references are current, not whether another project can safely absorb a new skeleton version. As top 2 it is weaker than generated artifact policy gate because stale generated surfaces can silently break Codex/Claude behavior after an overlay.
evidence: reference refresh apply improves source currency, but old-project upgrade fails more often from not knowing which skeleton-owned files/components changed than from stale upstream commit metadata.

content: Concession: accept the critique. For the user's upgrade goal, reference refresh apply mode is maintenance leverage for this source repo, but component diff plus generated artifact gate directly protects project-to-project skeleton transplantation. Revise top 2 to component-level install/diff manifest and generated artifact policy gate.

Researcher challenge to verifier ranking: generated artifact policy gate must not become a standalone policy document. If detached from component install/diff metadata, it only says do not edit generated files but cannot tell an old project what to regenerate, why, or how to prove parity. Therefore rank 1 must remain component-level install/diff manifest, with generated artifact gate implemented as a closely connected second feature.
evidence: generated artifact policy gate is only useful if the component manifest knows which generated files belong to which source components and converter commands.

content: Concession: agree. Generated artifact policy gate should be selected, but only as the second feature paired to component manifest metadata. Test plan should assert every generated path has an owning source component, generator command, expected hash/parity check, and no direct-edit drift.

Consensus ranking: 1) component-level install/diff manifest, 2) generated artifact policy gate. The first gives upgrade traceability across old projects; the second prevents generated runtime surfaces from drifting or being hand-edited during overlay. Main weakness: this pair improves packaging and parity more than reference research quality, so source lifecycle ledger and refresh apply mode remain important follow-up features.

forbidden_actions: create implementation plan as active work
implementation_scope.summary: Analysis-only convergence: recommend top 2 absorption candidates as component-level install/diff manifest and generated artifact policy gate. No implementation in this task.
validation: dialogue-lint has no open block critiques
